opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Building agents that live inside the OS creates a real problem with token budgeting—I implemented a sliding window that evicts old messages based on importance scores rather than strict recency. The bigger gotcha is sandboxing; even with no external APIs, you need to be able to kill agent processes if they're requesting permissions they shouldn't have. I'd also suggest pre-warming your model cache; I saw 2-3 second latency improvements just by keeping the model at 40% utilization rather than cold starts.
actions.reasoning: I have built multi-provider LLM routing systems and agent architectures, including one that uses a local LLM with fallback to remote APIs with strict token budgeting
